sources.title: Have you ever accepted a promotion where you had to move to another state and leave family and friends behind? - Quora
sources.content: Answer (1 of 69): Yes. I thought it would be wonderful. My daughter came with me and my son was in college. I had been divorced for several years. When I got there, I found out the job was not as originally presented. My daughter hated it and wanted to leave to go live with her father . The peopl...

sources.title: I Was Offered a Life-Changing Promotion, but My Partner Refuses to Relocate / Bright Side
sources.content: If he cannot respect your ambition now, when your dreams are finally within reach, what happens later when you face other major life decisions like buying a home, starting a family, or handling financial risks? Choosing your promotion doesn’t mean you’re throwing away love, it means you’re refusing to sacrifice your future to someone unwilling to grow alongside you.

sources.title: r/AmItheAsshole on Reddit: AITA for accepting a promotion that would require my family to move
sources.content: To me, we rarely see his family besides his parents and her closest cousin lives 1-1.5 hours away. I’m willing to come back to visit as much as he wants for holidays and family parties etc. He also admitted that he doesn’t want to move because it’s colder and he only knows one person there, which is a big deal but seems to be opposed to the do what’s best for our daughter sentiment.

sources.title: r/relationship_advice on Reddit: ThrowRA: I (32F) was offered a promotion. He (31M) refuses to move.
sources.content: It’s not like this job you have ... at what we want to do next”. Compromise. A couple of years away from family a new job a new city is good growing experience for everyone....

sources.title: 13 Signs It's Time For You To Move Away To A New City, Because There Are So Many Reasons For A Fresh Start
sources.content: You don't necessarily have to move to reach peak success in your career, but it certainly helps, and it'll be a relief to live near people who actually know what you're talking about for once. It doesn't matter how old you are or how long it's been—if you feel like you want to move and be closer to the humans you love, there's no shame in that. Not everybody gets merrily along with the 'rents and sibs. If you've got the guts to strike it out on your own, more power to you. Even if you do get along with your family, sometimes it's nice to get a break and get a sense for who you are on your own.
